name: Miyan Zogale (Moringa Soup)

ingredients.item: ground or chopped dawadawa (fermented locust beans)
1/2 cup roasted groundnut (ground to a smooth paste)

method: Place the moringa leaves in a pot, cover with 3 cups of boiling hot water and cook for 7-8 minutes on high heat. Drain through a strainer (the liquid can be saved and enjoyed as a highly nutritious tea) and rinse the leaves thoroughly under cold water. Set aside.

Heat the palm oil in a pot and add the chopped onions. Cook for 2-3 minutes on medium heat until the onions start to soften. Add the pepper sauce, 1 cup of water, yaji/suya spice, vegetable stock cube, dawadawa, groundnut paste, meat alternative of your choice and salt to taste. Cook for 12-15 minutes on medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the moringa leaves and simmer for another 5 minutes. Serve hot with tuwo shinkafa or tuwo masara.
